description: MADB first-run 'guided tour' seed. Plants a small, clearly-labelled causal DAG so a brand-new install produces a real recall + trace_cause moment on session #1 instead of an empty store. Every record is tagged 'onboarding_demo' so it can be cleanly removed after the tour (see onboarding/README.md).

memories.content: Sample project: samplex.io — a small SaaS that turns CSV uploads into shareable charts. Guiding constraints: ship fast and keep infrastructure cheap.
Decision: chose SvelteKit over Next.js for the samplex.io frontend. Reason: smaller bundle and less boilerplate for a small team shipping fast.
Decision: chose Cloudflare R2 + D1 over AWS S3 + RDS for samplex.io storage. Reason: no egress fees and a generous free tier honour the keep-infra-cheap constraint.

Fixed a hydration mismatch in the samplex.io chart component: the server rendered an empty canvas while the client drew data. Resolved by deferring the chart render to onMount so it only runs client-side.
After a public-bucket scare during testing, switched samplex.io uploads to short-lived signed URLs on R2 instead of public objects. Cheap to keep, and no accidental data exposure.

tour.suggested_recall_query: what do you remember about my project and the decisions I made
tour.expected_aha: recall returns the project context across a fresh session; trace_cause on the hydration fix walks back through the SvelteKit decision to the project root — lineage a flat vector store cannot produce.
first_prompts_for_user: What do you remember about my project?
Walk me back through how I ended up deferring the chart render to onMount.
